title: pseudopanax anomalus
intro: Pseudopanax anomalus, also known as the Kawakawa, is a native New Zealand plant species belonging to the family Araliaceae. It has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Pseudopanax anomalus include reducing pain and inflammation, as well as being used to treat skin conditions and wounds. Herbal preparations of Pseudopanax anomalus include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
